commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a Cursor-specific automatic menu-bar quota resolver, keeps MiniMax on constrained selection, adds resolver/status-item regression tests, and adds a changelog line.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main uses mostConstrainedWindow for Cursor automatic metrics, so a Total 67 / Auto 34 / API 100 snapshot can select the exhausted API lane instead of the usable overall quota.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer action is needed because the external PR still has mock-only proof and a stale body, which automation cannot repair for the contributor’s setup.

target.title: Fix menu bar quota for exhausted sublimits
target.body:
## Summary

- Skip an exhausted Cursor Auto/API subquota when another child quota remains usable.
- Keep Cursor's overall Total cap authoritative and retain 0% when every present child subquota is exhausted.
- Skip exhausted independent MiniMax service lanes when another service remains usable.
- Add resolver, menu-bar display, and highest-usage regression coverage.

## Review and proof

- Hosted CI exposed a real highest-usage regression in the initial head; the repaired resolver now treats Cursor Total separately from child subquotas.
- `swift test --filter 'MenuBarMetricWindowResolverTests|StatusItemBalanceDisplayTests|UsageStoreHighestUsageTests'`: 65 tests passed, including the previously red hosted case.
- `make check`: format and strict lint clean across 1,152 files.
- Full fixup autoreview: clean, confidence 0.84.

Exact candidate: `e9b8a4c94d37586edcd32659ced6f448488a6e4d`
target.author: yuxin-qiao
